title: 10 best herbal tinctures for lost voice

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower tinctures helps with lost voice because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the throat and vocal cords. The herb's antiviral and antibacterial compounds also help to combat infections that may be causing the loss of voice. Additionally, purple coneflower's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus, reducing inflammation and discomfort in the throat. This natural remedy can help to restore a healthy voice and promote vocal function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Purple coneflower contains compounds like quercetin, which can reduce inflammation in the vocal cords and surrounding tissues, helping to restore normal functioning and alleviate symptoms of hoarseness.

Antimicrobial properties: The tannins and phenolic acids present in purple coneflower have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections that may be contributing to a lost voice, such as laryngitis or tonsillitis.

Soothing and protective mucilage properties: Purple conefflower's mucilages can coat and soothe irritated mucous membranes in the throat, providing a protective barrier against further irritation and helping to calm inflammation, thus reducing discomfort and promoting healing.
remedy_constituents: Cichoric Acid: A phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, thereby aiding in the recovery of lost voice.

Ishaginin: A triterpene glycoside that has been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, which may help boost the immune system and reduce the severity of upper respiratory tract infections that can cause lost voice.

Caffeic Acid: A phenolic acid that has antioxidant properties, which may help protect the vocal cords from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by viral infections, thus promoting the recovery of lost voice.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea purpurea are commonly used to make tinctures for lost voice due to their rich content of bioactive compounds.
Flowers: Echinacea purpurea flowers are often used in tinctures to help soothe the throat and reduce inflammation, promoting a clear and strong voice.
Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea purpurea are used to make tinctures that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the throat, relieving symptoms of lost voice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea roots and flowers at dawn when moisture is lowest for best results in tincture making.
Chop 1 part of the harvested plant material by weight and combine with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka.
Steep the mixture in a clean glass jar for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass container discarding the solids after 1 hour.
Label and store the tincture in a cool dark place away from children and pets for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with lost voice because of its unique composition, which is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. The tannins present in the tincture have a soothing effect on the throat and vocal cords, reducing inflammation and calming irritation that can cause hoarseness or loss of voice. Additionally, the herb's natural expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the throat, allowing for easier breathing and speaking. As a result, using maidenhair tree tinctures can provide effective relief from lost voice and promote vocal restoration.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties can help protect the vocal cords from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to a loss of voice.
Anti-inflammatory Properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in maidenhair tree tincture can reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and vocal cords, helping to restore normal vocal function.
Vasodilatory Properties: Ginkgo biloba's vasodilatory effects can help increase blood flow to the throat and vocal cords, which may improve circulation and promote healthy vocal cord function.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids, a subgroup of phenolics, help alleviate lost voice by reducing inflammation and improving blood circulation to the vocal cords, which may help to restore normal vocal function.

Bilobalide: Bilobalide, a triterpenoid, may contribute to the recovery of lost voice by modulating the nervous system and reducing muscle spasms, which can help to relax the vocal cords and restore voice.

Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce swelling and oxidative stress in the vocal cords, promoting a faster recovery from lost voice.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have soothing and anti-inflammatory properties that can help restore vocal cord function.
Barks: The bark of the Ginkgo biloba tree is used for its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the throat and vocal cords, promoting a healthy voice.

Fruits: Ginkgo biloba fruits are used due to their content of ginkgolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to soothe and protect the vocal cords, promoting a clear and strong voice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 ginkgo biloba leaves from healthy trees in the fall season when they are fully ripe.
Clean the leaves thoroughly with a soft brush to remove dirt and debris from their surface.
Combine 1 part ginkgo biloba leaves with 2 parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another glass container, discarding the solids and retaining 20ml of the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with lost voice because of its unique combination of compounds that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat. The bioactive compounds, particularly glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling, allowing for a smoother vocal tract. Additionally, licorice tincture's demulcent qualities form a protective barrier on the mucosa, shielding it from irritation and further strain, enabling the voice to return to its normal pitch and tone with minimal discomfort.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and vocal cords, allowing for a smoother and more comfortable recovery from hoarseness or a lost voice.

Saponins: These compounds have antimicrobial properties, which help to combat infections that can cause loss of voice, such as laryngitis, and promote healing and restoration of normal vocal function.

Flavonoids: These antioxidants help to protect the throat and vocal cords from oxidative stress and damage caused by environmental toxins, allergens, or other irritants, which can contribute to a lost voice and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Glycyrrhizin, a saponin compound, has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, thereby alleviating vocal strain and promoting a healthy voice.

Flavonoids: Quercetin, a flavonoid present in Glycyrrhiza glabra, exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, allowing for smoother vocal cord movement and improved voice quality.

Terpenoids: 18α-Glycyrrhetinic acid, a terpenoid compound, has been found to have anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce throat irritation, inflammation, and swelling, ultimately contributing to the recovery of a lost or strained voice.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain high levels of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to soothe and protect the vocal cords, making it effective for lost voice.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant are also used to make tinctures for lost voice, as they contain similar compounds to the roots that help to calm and heal the vocal cords.

Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are sometimes used to make tinctures for lost voice, as they contain glycyrrhizin and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and chop them into small pieces for later use.
Combine the chopped roots with 3 parts of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks to steep.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Discard the solids and store the finished tincture in a cool dark place for up to 5 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm tinctures helps with lost voice because its mucilages soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, restoring a healthy balance of moisture and reducing inflammation. This natural remedy promotes healing and regeneration of damaged tissues, allowing the vocal cords to function properly once again. As a result, the voice becomes stronger, clearer, and more confident, with improved pitch and tone. Slippery elm tinctures provide gentle, non-irritating relief for irritated throats, making it an effective natural treatment for lost voice caused by illness, strain, or vocal abuse.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Slippery elm tincture contains mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that help soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with lost voice.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in slippery elm tincture can help reduce swelling and pain in the throat and vocal cords, allowing the voice to recover more quickly.

Emollients: The emollient properties of slippery elm tincture can help lubricate and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, promoting healthy healing and regeneration of damaged tissue, which can aid in the recovery of a lost voice.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help to reduce inflammation in the vocal cords and mucous membranes, allowing for smoother and more efficient vocal production.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as ellagic acid, exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and protect the vocal cords from damage, promoting a healthy and clear voice.

Tannins: Tannins in Ulmus rubra tinctures have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in the throat and vocal cords, allowing for improved vocal quality and reduced strain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Ulmus rubra are used for their demulcent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm and protect the mucous membranes of the throat.
Leaves: Ulmus rubra leaves are used due to their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to clear mucus and soothe the throat, promoting a healthy voice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ulmus rubra bark in early spring or late fall when it is most potent and aromatic.
Dry the harvested bark in a low-temperature oven at 100 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Chop the dried ulmus rubra bark into small pieces to increase its surface area for infusion.
Steep 1 part of chopped ulmus rubra bark in 2 parts of 80% ethanol for 2 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a strong herbal tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with lost voice because of its unique properties that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat. The anti-inflammatory compounds found in marshmallow root calm irritated tissues, reducing redness and swelling, which can occur when vocal cords are inflamed or strained. Additionally, the demulcent properties of marshmallow tincture create a protective barrier that helps to lock in moisture, keeping the throat hydrated and lubricated, allowing for smoother voice production and relief from hoarseness and lost voice.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow root contains thick, protective mucilages that soothe and lubricate the mucous membranes in the throat, relieving inflammation and reducing irritation caused by a lost voice.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in marshmallow tincture have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and discomfort in the vocal cords, allowing for smoother and more effective communication.

Demulcent action: Marshmallow's demulcent properties create a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, shielding them from further irritation and discomfort, while also helping to lock in moisture and soothe dry, irritated tissues.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages, a type of complex polysaccharide, help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat and vocal cords, reducing inflammation and promoting healing to recover from lost voice.

Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin): Quercetin, a flavonoid antioxidant, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and redness in the throat, allowing for smoother vocal cord vibrations and a return to a normal voice.

Saponins: Saponins, a type of glycoside, exhibit anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the mucous membranes, which can help calm and protect the vocal cords, reducing strain and allowing for vocal cord recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages that help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat.
Stems: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and irritation in the throat.
Leaves: Also contain mucilages, which can help to calm and heal the mucous membranes in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh roots of althaea officinalis, clean and chop them into small pieces, about 1 kg per 5 liters of solvent.
Combine the chopped roots with 5 liters of 80% ethanol or 75% glycerin in a large glass jar.
Place the jar in a cool dark place, allowing the mixture to macerate for 2-4 weeks, shaking occasionally.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles, label and store in a cool dark place to mature for 4-6 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Japanese honeysuckle tinctures helps with lost voice because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's flavonoids and saponins work together to soothe irritated vocal cords, reducing swelling and discomfort. This allows the voice to recover more quickly and regain its normal tone and volume. Additionally, Japanese honeysuckle has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to relieve coughs and sore throats, making it a natural remedy for soothing a hoarse or lost voice.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Japanese Honeysuckle's antiviral compounds may help combat viral infections that can cause loss of voice, such as laryngitis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The herb's anti-inflammatory compounds may reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and vocal cords, helping to alleviate discomfort and restore a healthy singing or speaking voice.

Expectorant properties: Japanese Honeysuckle's expectorant compounds can help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, including the lungs and throat, which may aid in relieving congestion and hoarseness associated with a lost voice.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help with lost voice by reducing inflammation and soothing the mucous membranes in the throat, promoting healing and recovery.
Phenolic acids (specifically, caffeic acid): Act as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing throat irritation and inflammation, and helping to protect the vocal cords.

Glycosides (specifically, lonicerine): Exert a soothing and protective effect on the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of the vocal cords, helping to restore a normal voice.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help calm the throat and restore vocal cord function.
Flowers: The flowers are rich in flavonoids and saponins, which are known to have anti-inflammatory and antitussive (cough-suppressing) effects, making them suitable for soothing a sore throat.
Stems: The stems of Lonicera japonica contain compounds that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the throat, which is beneficial for recovering a lost voice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh lonicera japonica flowers and leaves at the peak of their fragrance and potency.
Dry the harvested material in a warm place or using a dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Combine 200g of the dried lonicera japonica material with 1l of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and allow the mixture to macerate for 2-3 weeks shaking it occasionally.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container and discard the solids.

Rosmarinic acid: Possesses antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and protect the throat, vocal cords, and surrounding tissues, promoting a healthy voice.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thymus vulgaris leaves are commonly used to make tinctures for a lost voice due to their expectorant properties, which help relieve respiratory congestion.
Flowers: Thymus vulgaris flowers are used to make tinctures for a lost voice because they contain compounds that help soothe and protect the mucous membranes.
Barks: Thymus vulgaris barks are sometimes used to make tinctures for a lost voice, as they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that aid in reducing inflammation in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in the morning when they are at their highest oil content.
Dry the harvested thymus vulgaris in a single layer at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 1 to 2 hours.
Combine the dried thymus vulgaris with 2 cups of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar for 2 to 6 weeks.
Strain the thymus vulgaris and ethanol mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Store the thymus vulgaris tincture in a cool dark place for 2 to 4 weeks before using it.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with lost voice because of its natural soothing properties that can calm irritated vocal cords. The menthol in peppermint oil has a cooling effect on the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by vocal strain or illness. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as menthol and methyl salicylate, help to reduce swelling in the throat and vocal cords, alleviating discomfort and strain associated with a lost voice.

Expectorant properties: The menthol content in peppermint tincture helps to loosen and clear mucus from the throat, allowing for improved airflow and reducing congestion that can contribute to a hoarse or lost voice.

remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol helps with lost voice by providing a soothing and protective effect on the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid helps with lost voice by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, promoting healthy vocal cord function.

Limonene: Limonene helps with lost voice by displaying anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections and reduce inflammation in the throat, promoting a healthy environment for vocal cord function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of peppermint contain high concentrations of menthol and menthone, which are known for their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and calm the throat.
Flowers: The flowers of peppermint have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety, which can contribute to a lost voice, and their fragrance can help to open up airways.
Stems: The stems of peppermint contain menthol, which can help to thin mucus and reduce inflammation in the throat, making it easier to speak and regain a lost voice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of fresh menthaxpiperita leaves and flowers on a sunny morning after the dew has evaporated completely.
Clean the leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and debris from the surface of the plants.
Use a glass jar and combine 30g of the cleaned menthaxpiperita with 250ml of 45% ethanol in a 1:8 ratio by weight.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place and shake the jar gently for 2 weeks to allow infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another glass bottle discarding the solids and label the tincture clearly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with lost voice because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated vocal cords. The herbal remedy also possesses expectorant qualities, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the throat, allowing for easier communication and improved vocal function. remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds, particularly [6]-gingerol, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce throat inflammation and irritation, thereby soothing a sore throat and aiding in recovering a lost voice.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh root of zingiber officinale in early morning for highest volatile oil content.

Combine 1 part chopped root with 2 parts 80 proof vodka or 75% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily to facilitate infusion.

The curcumin present in turmeric also has antioxidant and antibacterial effects, helping to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes. remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, helps with lost voice by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the larynx, thus promoting healing and soothing irritated vocal cords.

Demethoxycurcumin: Demethoxycurcumin, another polyphenolic compound, helps with lost voice by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing swelling and discomfort in the vocal cords.

Volitin: Volitin, a sesquiterpene, helps with lost voice by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to the reduction of inflammation and oxidative stress in the larynx, ultimately promoting vocal cord recovery.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used because they contain a high concentration of curcumin, a bioactive compound that helps reduce inflammation and promote healing of the vocal cords.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh curcuma longa roots and dry them in a warm place for 7-10 days.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and combine with 500ml of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and steep for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to glass dropper bottles and store them in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with lost voice because they contain powerful anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe the mucous membranes in the throat and vocal cords. The tannins present in elderberries help to reduce inflammation, swelling, and pain, allowing the voice to regain its natural tone and pitch. Additionally, elder tinctures have antibacterial and antiviral properties that combat infections that may be causing the loss of voice, promoting a speedy recovery and alleviating discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and irritation in the vocal cords, allowing for smoother and more efficient vocal production.

remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and leaves at peak blooming for maximum potency.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours.
Grind 20 grams of dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Combine 1 part ground sambucus nigra with 2 parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, shaking well to combine.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle for 4-6 weeks at room temperature, shaking daily for optimal extraction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover tinctures helps with lost voice because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive compounds found in red clover, such as isoflavones and saponins, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the throat and vocal cords, thus alleviating symptoms associated with a lost voice. Additionally, red clover's expectorant properties help loosen mucus and promote its clearance from the respiratory tract, allowing for clearer communication and improved vocal function.
remedy_properties: Isolation of estrogen-like compounds: Red clover contains plant-based estrogens called isoflavones, which can help to soothe and calm irritated vocal cords, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy recovery of the voice.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in red clover tincture have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the throat and vocal cords, promoting healing and reducing discomfort.

Soften and lubricate mucous membranes: Red clover's saponins and flavonoids can help to soften and lubricate the mucous membranes in the throat and mouth, making it easier to speak and swallow, and reducing irritation and discomfort associated with a lost voice.

Leaves: The leaves of red clover contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and tension in the throat, allowing for easier speech.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh red clover flowers in the morning when they are fully open, collecting at least one pint for drying.
Dry the harvested red clover flowers using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours or by spreading them out in a thin layer on paper towels for several days.
Combine two cups of dried red clover flowers with 4 cups of high-proof vodka or everclear in a clean glass jar, sealing it tightly and storing it in a cool dark place for four weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the plant material before discarding the solids.

Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in the refrigerator at 40°f (4°c) for up to two years, using 20-30 drops of the tincture mixed with water three times a day to soothe an irritated throat and promote vocal recovery.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borage tinctures helps with lost voice because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm the throat and reduce swelling. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh borage flowers in morning dew for optimal potency and quality.
Clean and dry the harvested flowers thoroughly to remove any debris or moisture.
Combine one part of dried borage flowers with three parts of vodka or high-proof spirit in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction and infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and preserving the tincture for further use.

remedy_properties: Antiviral and antibacterial properties: Lemon balm's antiviral and antibacterial properties can help combat viral infections such as laryngitis, which can cause lost voice, by reducing the replication of the virus and killing bacteria that may be contributing to inflammation in the throat.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds, including rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, can help reduce swelling and irritation in the larynx and vocal cords, promoting healing and alleviating discomfort associated with lost voice.

Soothing and calming effects: Lemon balm's essential oils and flavonoids have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help relax the muscles in the throat and promote a sense of calm, reducing stress and anxiety that may exacerbate lost voice.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots are used in tinctures due to their calming and soothing properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, making them beneficial for alleviating a lost voice.
Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant contain high concentrations of essential oils that can help to ease throat irritation and soothe a sore throat, making them a popular choice for tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in mid-summer when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours or by air drying for several days.
Combine 100 grams of dried melissa officinalis with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and store in a cool dark place for 4 weeks.
After the infusion period strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into another glass container discarding the solids and label as melissa officinalis tincture.
Store the prepared tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature for up to one year and take 20-30 drops three times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with lost voice because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the throat. When we lose our voice, it's often due to inflammation or irritation in the vocal cords. Chamomile's calming effects reduce redness and swelling, allowing the vocal cords to relax and recover. Additionally, chamomile's antibacterial and antiviral properties help combat any underlying infections that may be contributing to the lost voice. By using herbal chamomile tinctures, individuals can quickly and naturally restore their lost voice.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile tincture helps reduce inflammation in the throat, which can irritate the vocal cords and contribute to a lost or hoarse voice.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in chamomile tincture help protect the delicate cells of the vocal cords from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy voice function and reducing strain on the vocal cords.

Soothing and calming property: Chamomile's calming effects can help relax the muscles of the throat and vocal cords, reducing tension and discomfort that may be contributing to a lost or hoarse voice.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Chamomile flowers have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them effective in calming the throat and reducing swelling caused by vocal cord damage or irritation.
Roots: Chamomile roots contain apigenin, an flavonoid that has been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the mucous membranes of the throat, helping to restore a lost voice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried flowers of matricaria chamomilla in quantities sufficient for the desired batch size.
Grind dried flowers into a coarse powder using mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Combine 1 part ground matricaria chamomilla with 2 parts high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks to allow for steeping and infusion.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle and label accordingly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with lost voice because its expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the vocal cords, allowing for improved airflow and vocal clarity. Additionally, fennel's natural anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and vocal folds, which can contribute to a lost or hoarse voice. As a result, herbal fennel tinctures provide natural relief and support for restoring a healthy, clear voice.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and irritation in the mucous membranes, which can cause a sore or lost voice.
Expectorant properties: Fennel tincture's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the throat and lungs, relieving congestion and coughing that can contribute to a lost voice.

Antibacterial properties: Fennel tincture's antibacterial properties help combat infections in the throat and respiratory tract, reducing the risk of bacterial invasion and further discomfort or complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are rich in mucilage, which helps soothe and calm irritated vocal cords.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain anethole, a natural expectorant that can help loosen and clear mucus from the throat, relieving hoarseness.
remedy_recipe: Gather organic foeniculum vulgare seeds in adequate quantity for the desired tincture strength.
Chop the harvested foeniculum vulgare seeds into small pieces and combine with 70% vodka or grain alcohol in a glass container.
Allow the mixture to macerate in a cool dark place for at least 6 weeks or until the desired extraction is achieved.
Strain the liquid from the solids through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the plant material.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and label with ingredients dosages and preparation date.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with lost voice because it is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that help soothe and heal the mucous membranes in the throat. The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties also reduce redness and swelling in the throat, allowing for clearer breathing and improved vocal function. Additionally, dandelion tincture's natural expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, further alleviating symptoms of lost voice. By promoting healthy throat tissues and reducing inflammation, dandelion tinctures can help restore a normal voice in no time.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's antioxidants help reduce inflammation in the mucous membranes, which can soothe a hoarse or lost voice caused by irritation or infection.
Expectorant properties: The saponins and flavonoids in dandelion tincture help loosen and clear mucus from the throat, reducing congestion and promoting a healthy tone of voice.

Immunomodulatory effects: Dandelion's immunomodulating compounds may help stimulate the immune system to respond more effectively to viral or bacterial infections that can cause lost voice, such as laryngitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots have been traditionally used to soothe and calm the throat, making it effective in treating loss of voice.
Buds: The buds of the dandelion plant contain high levels of antioxidants and flavonoids that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the throat, promoting a healthy recovery.
remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale roots at dawn on a dry day for optimal potency.
Wash the harvested roots under cold running water and pat dry with paper towels.
Chop the washed roots into small pieces and pack them tightly into a clean glass jar.
Add 80 proof vodka to cover the chopped roots by about an inch and seal the jar tightly.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 2 weeks and then strain through cheesecloth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tinctures helps with lost voice because its anti-inflammatory properties soothe the vocal cords, reducing inflammation and discomfort that can cause hoarseness. The natural antioxidants in stinging nettle also help to protect the delicate mucous membranes in the throat from further damage, promoting a healthy recovery. Additionally, stinging nettle's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear excess mucus from the respiratory tract, allowing for easier breathing and vocal production.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and irritation in the vocal cords, allowing for improved vocal function and reducing discomfort associated with lost voice.
Antioxidant properties: Nettle's antioxidants protect the delicate tissues of the larynx from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to hoarseness and loss of voice.

Vasodilatory properties: Stinging nettle's vasodilatory compounds help improve blood flow to the vocal cords, allowing for better oxygenation and nutrient delivery, which can help restore normal vocal function and alleviate a lost or hoarse voice.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated vocal cords, promoting a healthy voice.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and saponins, they have natural soothing and protective properties that aid in reducing inflammation and irritation in the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh nettle leaves and stems by cutting them in the early morning when dew is still present on the plants.
Rinse the harvested nettles thoroughly with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris from their surfaces.
Dry the rinsed nettles using a food dehydrator set at 55°c for 6 hours or until they reach a brittle texture.
Combine 1 part dried nettle leaves and stems with 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring all the botanical material is completely submerged in the liquid.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it daily to facilitate the extraction process before filtering the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with lost voice because it contains calendula, a natural anti-inflammatory that soothes irritated vocal cords. The herb's antiviral and antibacterial properties help to reduce swelling and congestion in the throat, allowing for smoother and more effortless speech. Additionally, pot marigold tincture's expectorant properties help to loosen mucus and promote clear breathing, which can be especially beneficial when dealing with a hoarse or lost voice caused by colds, allergies, or vocal strain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold contains flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling in the mucous membranes of the throat and vocal cords, allowing for a smoother and more comfortable transition back to normal voice use.

Antimicrobial properties: The tannins and phenolic acids present in pot marigold have antimicrobial effects, which can help combat infections that may be causing a lost or hoarse voice, such as laryngitis, by inhibiting the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi.

Soothing and protective properties: Pot marigold contains mucilages, which are substances that coat and protect the mucous membranes, providing relief from irritation, dryness, and inflammation in the throat and vocal cords, allowing for a more comfortable and effective recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Pot Marigold are used due to their ability to soothe and calm the throat, helping to alleviate hoarseness and restore a normal voice.

Seeds: The seeds of Pot Marigold are utilized in medicinal tinctures as they contain a high concentration of antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation in the throat and vocal cords, promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calendula flowers at peak bloom and quality for optimal potency.
Chop the calendula flowers into small pieces to increase surface area for infusion.
Combine chopped calendula with high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar filling the jar 75 percent with herbs.
Allow the mixture to macerate for six weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean dark glass bottle for storage.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for lost voice are natural remedies extracted from plants, herbs, and flowers that help to soothe and restore vocal cords. They work by reducing inflammation, calming irritation, and nourishing the throat, ultimately allowing for a smoother and clearer tone. Examples of herbal tinctures that can help with lost voice include slippery elm, licorice root, and thyme, which can be taken as a liquid extract or added to warm water as a tea. By using these tinctures, individuals can improve their vocal quality, regain confidence in their communication, and enhance their overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to lost voice that are treated with herbal tinctures are those affecting the throat and vocal cords. For example, sore throats, tonsillitis, and laryngitis can be relieved with tinctures made from herbs like slippery elm, echinacea, and goldenseal. Additionally, herbal tinctures of licorice root, marshmallow, and sage are used to soothe coughs and irritated throats, while those containing mullein and thyme may help reduce inflammation and congestion in the respiratory tract.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with lost voice is Throat Coat by Yogi, and a blend of Slippery Elm, Marshmallow, and Licorice root. Slippery Elm soothes and protects the mucous membranes, while Marshmallow reduces inflammation and Marshmallow aids in healing. Licorice root helps to reduce throat irritation and promote healing. Additionally, a small amount of Echinacea can be added to boost the immune system and speed up the recovery process. This blend can be taken sublingually or added to warm water as a soothing drink.
